actionItems.title: Review header resolution and quality flags
actionItems.summary: Review header alias resolution, row normalization, and any quality flags before using the monitoring interpretation bundle.

actionItems.title: Review occurrence evidence context
actionItems.summary: Confirm that governed occurrence-evidence records match the monitoring matrix, analyte, and interpretation scope.

actionItems.title: Review analytical method context
actionItems.summary: Confirm that governed analytical-method evidence remains appropriate for LOQ, recovery, storage, and uncertainty interpretation.

actionItems.title: Review linked focus records
actionItems.summary: Work through linked review-focus records for high-attention foods and sensitive populations referenced by the monitoring check.

actionItems.title: Review analytical measurement ledger entry
actionItems.summary: Row-level LOD coverage is incomplete. Keep the governed scientific ledger rationale explicit in reviewer signoff.

actionItems.title: Review occurrence data ledger entry
actionItems.summary: Lower-bound handling is governed by linked occurrence evidence. Keep the governed scientific ledger rationale explicit in reviewer signoff.

actionItems.title: Review analytical method ledger entry
actionItems.summary: Storage-stability interpretation relies on governed analytical-method context. Keep the governed scientific ledger rationale explicit in reviewer signoff.

actionItems.title: Review sampling context ledger entry
actionItems.summary: Sampling-plan interpretation relies on governed analytical-method context. Keep the governed scientific ledger rationale explicit in reviewer signoff.

actionItems.title: Review governance links
actionItems.summary: Confirm that source, method, legal, dataset, and reference-value links are sufficient for the intended monitoring review use.

Family-level evidence-maturity and submission-governance posture for this contaminant family.
Operator guide for reviewer-facing contaminant monitoring signoff packets.

notes: Signoff packet is a reviewer-facing overlay on top of the contaminant monitoring interpretation bundle and does not change the underlying bundle content.
Waived actions remain explicitly visible so downstream reviewers can inspect accepted deviations from the default contaminant monitoring review sequence.
Signed-off status records closure of the governed review workflow only; it does not certify scientific correctness, submission readiness, or regulatory approval.
Attached legal-limit review snapshots remain visible in signoff so partial, anchor-only, or missing jurisdiction support cannot be mistaken for a complete legal-limit layer.
At least one blocking contaminant monitoring action remains unresolved in the signoff packet.
